candidates.direction_name: Promote The Existing 256x128 Pivot Hot-Band Kernel
ranking_notes: This is the simplest direction that directly matches the new persistent human guidance around 256x128 tiling and 64x64 warp tiles.
It outranks dir_02 because it is already in-tree and therefore tests the geometry thesis without immediately paying the cost of a correctness-sensitive rewrite.
candidates.hypothesis: The 128x128 family has now delivered two incremental wins without moving achieved warps or occupancy limits. The cleanest next structural test is to route the default hot-band launch onto the existing 256x128 pivot kernel so the search directly tests the human-guided 256x128 / 64x64 tiling preference and a materially different CTA geometry on the accepted clean base.
candidates.expected_bottleneck: Current four-warp 128x128 CTA geometry is capping residency and CTA-count efficiency on the hot-band region more than local pointer arithmetic is.
candidates.risk: Moderate. The kernel already exists and aligns with the human tiling guidance, but a larger CTA can still lose if it bloats the live set or export path.
metrics_to_recheck: kernel name and grid size in ncu_metrics.csv
candidates.stop_condition: Stop if the 256x128 pivot kernel fails to improve achieved warps or regresses runtime materially versus the new accepted base.
expected_local_changes: Swap the default hot-band launch from the current 128x128 regular sibling to the existing 256x128 pivot kernel.
Keep the residual row-band and tail handling unchanged for the first measurement.

candidates.direction_name: Transplant The Half-Panel Register Budget Into The Correct 256x128 Pivot
ranking_notes: This is the strongest direct match to the human guidance around 256x128 tiling, register reuse, and deeper buffering.
It ranks behind dir_01 only because the geometry-only pivot promotion is a much cleaner first test on the same idea axis.
candidates.hypothesis: If the simple 256x128 pivot is still not enough, the stronger version of the same human-guided idea is to start from the correctness-safe 256x128 pivot and transplant the low-register staging / reuse behavior from the archived half-panel branch without reviving split-writer ownership.
candidates.expected_bottleneck: Register footprint and steady-state staging efficiency inside the 256x128 hot-band path are still capping residency and latency hiding.
candidates.risk: High. This is the highest-ceiling family left in the repo, but it touches the same 256x128 control/export surface that has failed correctness before.
metrics_to_recheck: correctness on all 3 dataset cases before trusting runtime
candidates.stop_condition: Stop immediately if correctness fails again or if the implementation revives the old split-writer ownership pattern.
expected_local_changes: Start from the correctness-safe 256x128 pivot launch surface.
Transplant only the low-register staging and reuse ideas from the archived half-panel branch.

candidates.direction_name: Trim Microkernel Barriers Without Reintroducing Shared-Memory Blowup
ranking_notes: This stays in the top three because it is the strongest non-256x128 fallback and still matches the human guidance around async copy, reuse, and staging.
It ranks third because the new accepted 128x128 run suggests the next big move should test geometry before returning to another PTX schedule surgery.
candidates.hypothesis: If the 256x128 family underdelivers, the best orthogonal fallback is still to return to the accepted PTX microkernel surface and trim the single-K barrier cadence more surgically while preserving the smaller shared-memory footprint.
candidates.expected_bottleneck: Barrier cadence and PTX-stage handoff overhead inside the single-K 128x128 microkernel remain an unresolved latency tax on the accepted PTX surface.
candidates.risk: High. This remains PTX-microkernel surgery on the accepted surface.
candidates.stop_condition: Stop if shared memory rises above the accepted 22,016 B footprint or if barrier stall does not improve materially when compared to the PTX accepted-base profile.
expected_local_changes: Restore the accepted PTX microkernel launch surface if needed before editing the wait/sync cadence.
Retune the single-K consumer-barrier handoff without adding a second full K-tile shared buffer pair.

notes: Projected exactly three node_b directions into normalized search candidates.
